dashboard.label: Setup Guide
dashboard.description: Getting started with VDConnect for Splunk

div.p: Collect, index, and search logs stored inside vector databases.
Version 1.0.0 · Supports Milvus, Pinecone, Weaviate, ChromaDB, Qdrant, pgvector

div.style: background:#1a1d23; border:1px solid #2a2d35; border-radius:10px; padding:24px 28px; margin-bottom:16px;
div.p: VDConnect requires Python SDK libraries for each vector database you plan to connect to. Install them into the app's lib/ directory:

pre.style: background:#12141a; padding:14px 18px; border-radius:8px; color:#2DDE98; font-size:12px; overflow-x:auto;

div.pre:
# Milvus pip install pymilvus --target=$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/vdconnect/lib
# Pinecone pip install pinecone-client --target=$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/vdconnect/lib
# Weaviate pip install weaviate-client --target=$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/vdconnect/lib
# ChromaDB pip install chromadb-client --target=$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/vdconnect/lib
# Qdrant pip install qdrant-client --target=$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/vdconnect/lib
# pgvector (uses psycopg2) pip install psycopg2-binary --target=$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/vdconnect/lib

div.style: background:#1a1d23; border:1px solid #2a2d35; border-radius:10px; padding:24px 28px; margin-bottom:16px;
div.p: VDConnect ships with recommended indexes in default/indexes.conf. Verify they are created:
div.pre: | rest /services/data/indexes | search title=vdconnect_* | table title currentDBSizeMB maxTotalDataSizeMB

div.style: background:#1a1d23; border:1px solid #2a2d35; border-radius:10px; padding:24px 28px; margin-bottom:16px;
div.p: Copy the example stanzas from default/inputs.conf into local/inputs.conf and customize for your environment:

pre.style: background:#12141a; padding:14px 18px; border-radius:8px; color:#00A1EA; font-size:12px; overflow-x:auto;

div.pre: [vdconnect://my_milvus_logs] db_type = milvus host = milvus-prod.company.com port = 19530 auth_type = token token = your_token_here collection_name = error_logs output_fields = log_text, timestamp, severity, source collection_mode = rising_column rising_column = timestamp interval = 300 index = vdconnect_logs sourcetype = vdconnect:milvus disabled = false

div.style: background:#1a1d23; border:1px solid #2a2d35; border-radius:10px; padding:24px 28px; margin-bottom:16px;
div.p: Restart Splunk to load the new input, then verify data is flowing:
div.pre:
splunk restart
# Verify inputs are registered | rest /services/data/inputs/vdconnect | table title db_type disabled
# Check for collected events index=vdconnect_logs sourcetype=vdconnect:milvus | head 20
# Check internal logs for errors index=vdconnect_internal sourcetype=vdconnect:internal level=ERROR

div.style: background:#1a1d23; border:1px solid #2a2d35; border-radius:10px; padding:24px 28px; margin-bottom:16px;
div.p: Common searches to get started with your vector database logs:
div.pre: index=vdconnect_logs sourcetype=vdconnect:* severity=ERROR earliest=-24h | stats count by sourcetype, source | sort - count
index=vdconnect_logs OR index=vdconnect_audit sourcetype=vdconnect:* | eval db = replace(sourcetype, "vdconnect:", "") | timechart span=1h count by db
index=vdconnect_logs sourcetype=vdconnect:milvus severity=ERROR | top limit=20 log_text
